front: What are the two primary components of a full stack application?
back: The front-end (client-side) and the back-end (server-side). The front-end is what users interact with, while the back-end handles data processing, storage, and business logic.

front: Define the role of a front-end developer.
back: A front-end developer focuses on building the user interface and client-side functionality of a web application using core technologies like HTML (structure), CSS (styling), and JavaScript (interactivity).

front: What is the primary responsibility of a back-end developer?
back: A back-end developer builds and maintains the server, application, and database that power the front-end. They work with server-side languages (e.g., Node.js, Python), APIs, and databases to ensure data is processed and delivered correctly.

front: What is an API and what is its purpose in full stack development?
back: API stands for Application Programming Interface. It is a set of rules and protocols that allows different software applications (like the front-end and back-end) to communicate and exchange data with each other.

front: What is a technology stack? Give a common example.
back: A technology stack is a combination of software products and programming languages used to create a full stack application. A common example is the MERN stack: MongoDB (database), Express.js (back-end framework), React (front-end library), and Node.js (JavaScript runtime environment).
